channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lunar Lander Touches Down on Moon, Latest Step in Private Business Venture
item.description:
Private lunar lander carrying NASA experiments touches down on moon, marking latest step in private business venture ahead of astronaut missions." char='UTF-8'>

Historic Landing: Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost Lander Touches Down on Moon

A private lunar lander carrying a drill, vacuum and other experiments for NASA touched down on the moon Sunday, marking the latest step in a string of companies looking to kickstart business on Earth's celestial neighbor ahead of astronaut missions.

Firefly Aerospace’s Blue Ghost lander descended from lunar orbit on autopilot, aiming for the slopes of an ancient volcanic dome in an impact basin on the moon’s northeastern edge of the near side.

Confirmation of successful touchdown came from the company's Mission Control outside Austin, Texas, following the action some 225,000 miles (360,000 kilometers) away.

“You all stuck the landing. We’re on the moon,” Firefly’s Will Coogan, chief engineer for the lander, reported.

Private Space Companies Take Center Stage

A half hour after landing, Blue Ghost started to send back pictures from the surface, the first one a selfie somewhat obscured by the sun's glare. The second shot included the home planet, a blue dot glimmering in the blackness of space.

Two other companies’ landers are hot on Blue Ghost’s heels, with the next one expected to join it on the moon later this week.

The moon is littered with wreckage not only from Firefly, but dozens of other failed attempts over the decades.

NASA's Commercial Lunar Delivery Program

Launched in mid-January from Florida, the lander carried 10 experiments to the moon for NASA. The space agency paid $101 million for the delivery, plus $44 million for the science and tech on board.

It’s the third mission under NASA’s commercial lunar delivery program, intended to ignite a lunar economy of competing private businesses while scouting around before astronauts show up later this decade.

Private Lunar Landers Face Unique Challenges

Unlike NASA’s successful Apollo moon landings that had billions of dollars behind them and ace astronauts at the helm, private companies operate on a limited budget with robotic craft that must land on their own.

“It really does open up a whole new way for us to get more science to space and to the moon," NASA's top science officer Nicky Fox said.

Next Steps in Lunar Exploration

Another lander — a tall and skinny 15-footer (4 meters tall) built and operated by Houston-based Intuitive Machines — is due to land on the moon Thursday. It’s aiming for the bottom of the moon, just 100 miles (160 kilometers) from the south pole.

A third lander from the Japanese company ispace is still three months from landing. It shared a rocket ride with Blue Ghost from Cape Canaveral on Jan. 15, taking a longer, windier route.

NASA wants to keep up a pace of two private lunar landers a year, realizing some missions will fail, said Fox.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tired of overdramatic TikTok food influencers? Professional critics are too
item.description: TikTok and Instagram are flooded with reels of food influencers hyping already viral restaurants or bringing hundreds of thousands of eyes to hidden gems.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: The people who once ran the federal weather and oceans agency say it touches people’s daily lives in unnoticed ways and that massive firings there could cause needless deaths and a big hit to America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hollywood’s obsession with AI-enabled ‘perfection’ is making movies less human
item.description: The notion of authenticity in the movies has moved a step beyond the merely realistic. More and more, expensive and time-consuming fixes to minor issues of screen realism have become the work of statistical data renderings—the visual or aural products of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: A.S. Hamrah, Fast Company

item.title: Santa Cruz-based Joby Aviation expects first air taxi passengers in late 2025, early 2026
item.description: The company also reported it received more than $1 billion of additional funding and commitments in the fourth quarter.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Autonomous robotaxi successfully completes 75 mph highway testing
item.description: Motional is developing autonomous vehicles capable of handling a wide range of real-world scenarios. Its high-speed robotaxi testing reached speeds of 75 mph.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift improves virtualization support
item.description: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18, the latest version of the company’s Kubernetes-powered application platform, has arrived, with enhancements designed to simplify management of virtual machines and containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Plug-and-play web development with Astro
item.description: Astro is a full-stack JavaScript meta-framework that orchestrates reactive view technologies like React and Svelte. While there are numerous meta-frameworks (examples include Next.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives
item.description: Agencies’ attention and efforts are now shifting towards strengthening cybersecurity defenses to address the more challenging aspects of cyber threats. The post Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Review: Gemini Code Assist is good at coding
item.description: Given that Microsoft/GitHub/Azure and Amazon/AWS offer AI coding assistants, namely GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer, which work as add-ins to Visual Studio Code and other IDEs, it was inevitable that Google/GCP would introduce one of its own.
item.dc:creator: Info World
